Package i5.cb.graph

Class GraphMenu

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • javax.swing.AbstractButton
            • javax.swing.JMenuItem
              • javax.swing.JMenu
                • i5.cb.graph.GraphMenu
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ILangChangeable, java.io.Serializable

    public class GraphMenu
extends javax.swing.JMenu
implements ILangChangeable, java.io.Serializable
    The main purpose of this extension of JMenu is the implementation of the ILangChangeable interface. To create an instance of this class, you have to specify the name of a ResourceBundle and a keyWord that is handled in this Bundle.
    Since:
    1.0
    See Also:
    JMenu, ILangChangeable, ResourceBundle, Serialized Form

    • Constructor Detail

      • GraphMenu

        public GraphMenu​(java.lang.String keyWord,
                 java.lang.String bundleName,
                 char mnemonic)
        Creates a JMenu that reacts on the given mnemonic key. Gets the basic resource textBundle and labeles the Menu with the String, that is specified by the keyWord.
        Parameters:
        keyWord - make sure, that this String is a valid keyWord of the given resourceBundle.
        bundleName - name of the resourceBundle baseclass
        mnemonic - the menu listens to this keyInput

      • GraphMenu

        public GraphMenu​(java.lang.String keyWord,
                 java.lang.String bundleName)
        Creates a JMenu. Gets the basic resource textBundle and labeles the Menu with the String, that is specified by the keyWord.
        Parameters:
        keyWord - a String value
        bundleName - a String value

    • Method Detail

      • getItemByKeyWord

        public GraphMenuItem getItemByKeyWord​(java.lang.String keyWord)
        If you do not know the position of a GraphMenuItem, you can acces it by its keyWord.
        Parameters:
        keyWord - the keyWord of the wanted GraphMenuItem
        Returns:
        the GraphMenuItem or null, if it was not found

      • getSubMenuByKeyWord

        public GraphMenu getSubMenuByKeyWord​(java.lang.String keyWord)
        If you do not know the position of a GraphMenuItem, you can acces it by its keyWord.
        Parameters:
        keyWord - the keyWord of the wanted GraphMenuItem
        Returns:
        the GraphMenuItem or null, if it was not found

      • updateLang

        public javax.swing.text.DefaultStyledDocument updateLang​(java.util.Locale loc)
        Updates the textField and all subMenuItems if they implement the ILangChangeable interface. Requires, that this GraphMenu's keyWord is defined in the current textBundle.
        Specified by:
        updateLang in interface ILangChangeable
        Parameters:
        loc - the new locale
        Returns:
        always null at the moment
